title: How to connect boards wisely?
excerpt: I am managing data of drone flighs. In one board there is a table which contains the flight data. The columns of this table are: drone number and flight date. I want to create a summarizing table in a new board which con…

posts.author: Koby.Bar (Koby Bar)
posts.cookedHtml: I am managing data of drone flighs. In one board there is a table which contains the flight data. The columns of this table are: drone number and flight date. I want to create a summarizing table in a new board which contains the following data: drone numer, starting date, number of flights. number of flights should count the number of flights of the specific drone number since ‘starting date’ time. please advice me how to do so step by step.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Koby.Bar,

You can use connected boards column to connect between which the boards contain the relevant information and then sync the data to your summary board.

We’ve developed an app called Connected Boards Automations. Which allows to run robust automations on connected boards column. and achieve this exact use case. Here’s how →

Automatically connect items between boards →

Screenshot 2024-08-06 at 16.24.262300×392 27.2 KB

Sync the data between them of the relevant columns →

Screenshot 2024-07-26 at 15.43.462358×406 22.2 KB

You can find it in the monday.com app marketplace in the platform or here - Connected Boards Automations - monday.com App Marketplace

Hope that helps!
